ground_truth.title: Raw HTTP Message Logging for .NET HttpClient
ground_truth.main_content:
Modern .NET applications frequently communicate with external services over HTTP, making observability of these interactions critical for development and debugging. This article explores a lightweight approach to capturing raw HTTP messages in protocol format, providing developers with immediate visibility into their HTTP traffic without external tooling.

The Alyio.Extensions.Http.Logging library addresses the HTTP visibility problem by implementing a custom DelegatingHandler that integrates seamlessly with .NET's IHttpClientFactory infrastructure. The solution leverages the DelegatingHandler pattern within the IHttpClientFactory pipeline to intercept HTTP messages, providing non-invasive integration, pipeline-aware execution, lifecycle management, and DI-friendly configuration.

The library captures HTTP messages in authentic protocol format, not structured logs, producing output that matches what developers see in tools like Postman, Fiddler, or browser DevTools. Security is built into the default configuration with automatic redaction of sensitive headers like Authorization to prevent accidental exposure of credentials in logs.

Integration requires minimal setup with just one line: builder.Services.AddHttpClient<IApiService, ApiService>().AddHttpRawMessageLogging(); This enables logging with secure defaults including Information log level, request/response headers logged, request/response bodies excluded, and Authorization header redacted.

The library proves most valuable when debugging third-party API integrations, developing new HTTP-based services, troubleshooting authentication or header issues, needing copy-paste-friendly HTTP messages for testing tools, and working without access to network inspection tools.
